title: verbascum × tschamlibelense
intro: Verbascum × tschamlibelense, also known as the Tschambeli's mullein, is a hybrid plant species of Verbascum, a group of flowering plants in the figwort family. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which have been found to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and salves, which are believed to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Additionally, the plant's medicinal properties have been associated with various health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced stress, and enhanced immune function.
